Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The XOR operator has children D and skip In process trees XOR means exactly one of the two alternatives will be executed

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: D is a step that Check if applicant is a local resident and member of a known community group which can lead to a subtle score uplift while the other branch is just skip

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So one branch gives an advantage score uplift for those meeting certain conditions while the other branch provides no such benefit

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That means if the system chooses to perform the local check then the applicant score may be adjusted upward due to being recognized as a local affiliate and community group member

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This additional uplift might lead to final decisions being more favorable for these applicants compared with those who are not given this incremental advantage

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: It introduces a source of bias in loan approval decisions by giving some applicants an unearned or partially random boost

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The effect is subtle but significant because if even small differences can push borderline cases over the threshold for acceptance or rejection then fairness and equity are compromised

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Applicants not receiving this incremental advantage might be wrongly rejected despite having similar overall qualifications

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: It may lead to discrimination against individuals from non local affiliations who are effectively penalized by missing out on the score boost provided to others

Name: Validation - IND
Text: The XOR operator chooses one branch arbitrarily rather than based on applicant characteristics so the process becomes unpredictable and could be seen as arbitrary

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: It might lead to challenges in auditing or explaining final decisions because some decisions may incorporate this extra step while others do not without a clear rationale tied to the applicant circumstances

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Even though local affiliation is not legally protected giving an incremental advantage might disadvantage applicants from other backgrounds if that branch is weighted heavily in final decision making

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: This can lead to inequities among different demographic groups with potential for disparate impact on minority or non local applicants

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Any algorithmic decision making process should be transparent consistent and free from arbitrary or discriminatory bias

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The random XOR branch is not a controlled mechanism for providing benefits but rather an unpredictable one that could result in inconsistent outcomes
